dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: My son!

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Here I am

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I am old; I do not know the day of my death

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Now then, take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: And make me savory food, such as I love, and bring it to me that I may eat, so that my soul may bless you before I die

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I heard your father speaking to your brother Esau, saying:

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Bring me game and make me savory food, that I may eat and bless you before YHWH before my death

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice as I command you

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Go to the flock and bring me two good young goats, so that I may prepare from them savory food for your father, such as he loves

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: And you shall bring it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before his death

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall seem to be mocking him and bring a curse upon myself and not a blessing

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Let your curse be on me, my son; only obey my voice, and go

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: My father!

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Here I am. Who are you, my son?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I am Esau your firstborn. I have done as you told me; now sit up and eat of my game, that your soul may bless me

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Because YHWH your God granted me success

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, whether you are really my son Esau or not

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Are you really my son Esau?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I am

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Bring it near to me that I may eat of my son's game, so that my soul may bless you

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Come near and kiss me, my son

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: See, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field that YHWH has blessed

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: May God give you of the dew of heaven and of the fatness of the earth and plenty of grain and wine

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers, and may your mother's sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you, and blessed be everyone who blesses you

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Let my father arise and eat of his son's game, that your soul may bless me

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Who are you?

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: I am your son, your firstborn, Esau

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate it all before you came, and I have blessed him? Indeed he shall be blessed

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Bless me, even me also, O my father!

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Your brother came deceitfully and took away your blessing

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times. He took away my birthright, and behold, now he has taken away my blessing

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Have you not reserved a blessing for me?

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have made him lord over you, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants, and with grain and wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Have you but one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, O my father!

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Behold, away from the fatness of the earth shall your dwelling be, and away from the dew of heaven on high

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother; but when you grow restless you shall break his yoke from your neck

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Behold, your brother Esau comforts himself about you by planning to kill you

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice. Arise, flee to Laban my brother in Haran

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: And stay with him a few days, until your brother's fury turns away

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Until your brother's anger turns away from you, and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send and fetch you from there. Why should I be bereft of you both in one day?

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: I am weary of my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women such as these, one of the women of the land, what good will my life be to me?
